bullets: Automated trading system for the XAUUSD gold spot market that layers smart money concepts and multi-timeframe structural alignment; sample size, period, and out-of-sample split are not stated.

A locally deployed Qwen-2.5 14B model auto-classifies each session into one of six regimes and dispatches regime-specific chain-of-thought prompts to authorize or block trades, with no external validation reported.
Reports a 40 percent win rate paired with a 1:3 to 1:4 risk-to-reward floor, which the author argues yields positive expected value per trade.
validation_note: backtest win rate only, no ground-truth benchmark

bullets: Experimental study of causal caution, the propensity to withhold causal judgment under weak evidence, run across 480 trials contrasting academic prompts with practical business and policy advisory prompts.
Four models, Claude Sonnet 4.6, Claude Opus 4.7, GPT-5.5, and Gemini 3.1 Pro, were scored with a rubric based on Pearl's causal hierarchy, with no external ground truth used.
Caution maintenance fell from 91.7 to 100 percent in academic contexts to 6.7 to 18.3 percent in practical ones, and a short reconsideration prompt restored it to 71.4 to 100 percent.

Framework and proposed experimental design for extracting risk factors from annual reports, management discussion and analysis sections, and regulatory filings; the specific sample and period are not stated.

Transformer-based language models combined with retrieval augmentation and financial ontologies extract and classify risk factors; the paper names no specific model and reports no accuracy or agreement figure.

Reports that generative AI improves extraction accuracy and classification over conventional machine learning, and is particularly effective at identifying emerging risks embedded in narrative disclosures.
validation_note: improvement claimed but no ground-truth accuracy reported
